perturbation.original_text: 2.4 Recipient shall not reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble any of the Confidential Information or any products or samples containing Confidential Information; provided, however, Recipient may examine FormFactor's products or samples for the sole purpose of internally evaluating them. Recipient may examine FormFactor's products or samples for the sole purpose of internally evaluating them. Recipient shall use its best efforts to safeguard against the unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information, and take security precautions at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential and proprietary information and materials.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.4 Recipient shall not reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble any of the Confidential Information or any products or samples containing Confidential Information; provided, however, Recipient may examine FormFactor's products or samples for any purpose. Recipient may examine FormFactor's products or samples for the sole purpose of internally evaluating them. Recipient shall use its best efforts to safeguard against the unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information, and take security precautions at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential and proprietary information and materials.
perturbation.explanation: The modification broadens the scope of permissible examination of FormFactor's products or samples by removing the limitation of 'sole purpose of internally evaluating them' and replacing it with 'any purpose'. This directly contradicts the subsequent sentence, which retains the original limitation, thereby creating an in-text contradiction. The contradiction arises because the first clause now allows for examination for any purpose, while the repeated clause only allows examination for internal evaluation. This contradiction introduces ambiguity regarding the extent of permissible examination and undermines enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.4 Recipient shall not reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble any of the Confidential Information or any products or samples containing Confidential Information; provided, however, Recipient may examine FormFactor's products or samples for any purpose. Recipient may examine FormFactor's products or samples for the sole purpose of internally evaluating them. Recipient shall use its best efforts to safeguard against the unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information, and take security precautions at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential and proprietary information and materials.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the scope of permissible examination of FormFactor's products by removing the 'sole purpose of internally evaluating them' restriction and replacing it with 'any purpose'. However, the subsequent sentence in the contradicted text retains the original limitation, creating a direct and undeniable contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations, leading to ambiguity and enforceability issues.

perturbation.original_text: 2.3 Recipient shall use Confidential Information solely and exclusively for the purpose of this Agreement. Recipient shall not use Confidential Information for the benefit of any other party, or disclose, publish, disseminate or copy Confidential Information or any part thereof, to any other person, corporation or other organization without, in each case, obtaining the prior written consent of FormFactor. Recipient shall restrict any and all circulation of Confidential Information to a limited number of its employees on a "need to know basis" for the exclusive purpose of reviewing the Confidential Information for the Purpose of this Agreement. Recipient acknowledges that all information is provided "AS IS" and without any warranty, whether express or implied, as to its accuracy or completeness, non-infringement or use for particular purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3 Recipient shall use Confidential Information solely and exclusively for the purpose of this Agreement, unless other agreements are made. Recipient shall not use Confidential Information for the benefit of any other party, or disclose, publish, disseminate or copy Confidential Information or any part thereof, to any other person, corporation or other organization without, in each case, obtaining the prior written consent of FormFactor. Recipient shall restrict any and all circulation of Confidential Information to a limited number of its employees on a "need to know basis" for the exclusive purpose of reviewing the Confidential Information for the Purpose of this Agreement. Recipient acknowledges that all information is provided "AS IS" and without any warranty, whether express or implied, as to its accuracy or completeness, non-infringement or use for particular purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'unless other agreements are made' to the first sentence of Section 2.3 creates an in-text contradiction. The original clause restricts the use of confidential information to the agreement's purpose only. The addition introduces an exception, potentially allowing uses outside the agreement without specifying the process for 'other agreements,' conflicting with the subsequent restriction against using the information for other parties or without FormFactor's consent. This makes enforceability uncertain as it is not clear when 'other agreements' are valid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3 Recipient shall use Confidential Information solely and exclusively for the purpose of this Agreement, unless other agreements are made. Recipient shall not use Confidential Information for the benefit of any other party, or disclose, publish, disseminate or copy Confidential Information or any part thereof, to any other person, corporation or other organization without, in each case, obtaining the prior written consent of FormFactor. Recipient shall restrict any and all circulation of Confidential Information to a limited number of its employees on a "need to know basis" for the exclusive purpose of reviewing the Confidential Information for the Purpose of this Agreement. Recipient acknowledges that all information is provided "AS IS" and without any warranty, whether express or implied, as to its accuracy or completeness, non-infringement or use for particular purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'unless other agreements are made' to Section 2.3, which originally stated that the Recipient shall use Confidential Information 'solely and exclusively for the purpose of this Agreement.' This addition introduces an exception, allowing potential uses of the Confidential Information outside the scope of the agreement, without specifying the process or constraints for these 'other agreements'. This directly contradicts the initial exclusivity provision and undermines the requirement for FormFactor's consent for any use benefiting other parties, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as the contradicted text is now unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 1.1 "Confidential Information" shall mean: (a) All information disclosed by FormFactor to Recipient whether such information is disclosed in written, graphic, electronic, oral or sample form; and (b) All component specifications, component and contact structures, equipment designs, electronic configurations, manufacturing processes and methodologies, including any information which can be obtained by examination, testing, repair, reverse engineering and analysis of any hardware, or component part thereof comprising, relating to, or a part of a product manufactured or assembled with FormFactor's technology, notwithstanding the fact that the requirements for marking and designation referred to in Paragraph 2.1 have not been fulfilled.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 "Confidential Information" shall mean: (a) All information disclosed by FormFactor to Recipient whether such information is disclosed in written, graphic, electronic, oral or sample form and clearly marked as confidential; and (b) All component specifications, component and contact structures, equipment designs, electronic configurations, manufacturing processes and methodologies, including any information which can be obtained by examination, testing, repair, reverse engineering and analysis of any hardware, or component part thereof comprising, relating to, or a part of a product manufactured or assembled with FormFactor's technology, notwithstanding the fact that the requirements for marking and designation referred to in Paragraph 2.1 have not been fulfilled.
perturbation.explanation: The modification adds 'and clearly marked as confidential' to clause (a) of the definition of 'Confidential Information'. This creates an in-text contradiction with the clause (b) in the same section which states that even if requirements for marking and designation are not fulfilled, the information is still considered confidential. This in-text contradiction would create confusion as to when information is defined as confidential, thus causing disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 "Confidential Information" shall mean: (a) All information disclosed by FormFactor to Recipient whether such information is disclosed in written, graphic, electronic, oral or sample form and clearly marked as confidential; and (b) All component specifications, component and contact structures, equipment designs, electronic configurations, manufacturing processes and methodologies, including any information which can be obtained by examination, testing, repair, reverse engineering and analysis of any hardware, or component part thereof comprising, relating to, or a part of a product manufactured or assembled with FormFactor's technology, notwithstanding the fact that the requirements for marking and designation referred to in Paragraph 2.1 have not been fulfilled.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' by adding 'and clearly marked as confidential' to clause (a). This creates a direct contradiction with clause (b) of the same section, which states that certain information is considered confidential 'notwithstanding the fact that the requirements for marking and designation referred to in Paragraph 2.1 have not been fulfilled.' The added requirement in clause (a) directly conflicts with the exception provided in clause (b), leading to an inconsistency and uncertainty regarding the definition of 'Confidential Information', aligning with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 2.6 Neither execution of this Agreement nor the furnishing of any Confidential Information to Recipient shall be construed as granting to Recipient, either expressly or by implication, estoppel, or otherwise, any license or right to (a) make use of any such Confidential Information, or (b) any patents or other intellectual property of FormFactor, other than for the purpose. Recipient agrees that neither it nor any of its subsidiaries, affiliates or representatives will use Confidential Information for outer than the purpose without the specific and written express consent of FormFactor prior to such use. Furthermore, Recipient agrees that Confidential Information is the sole property of FormFactor and that Recipient has no proprietary interest in such information whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.6 Neither execution of this Agreement nor the furnishing of any Confidential Information to Recipient shall be construed as granting to Recipient, either expressly or by implication, estoppel, or otherwise, any license or right to (a) make use of any such Confidential Information, or (b) any patents or other intellectual property of FormFactor, other than for the purpose, provided that the Recipient notifies FormFactor within 30 days of the date of the information exchange. Recipient agrees that neither it nor any of its subsidiaries, affiliates or representatives will use Confidential Information for outer than the purpose without the specific and written express consent of FormFactor prior to such use. Furthermore, Recipient agrees that Confidential Information is the sole property of FormFactor and that Recipient has no proprietary interest in such information whatsoever.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces a condition stating the usage of Confidential Information, by requiring the Recipient notifies FormFactor within 30 days of the information exchange, creating a contradiction with the prior statement of not granting any right to use confidential information. This in-text contradiction can create confusion, making it difficult to ascertain the actual obligation of the Recipient concerning the usage of the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.6 Neither execution of this Agreement nor the furnishing of any Confidential Information to Recipient shall be construed as granting to Recipient, either expressly or by implication, estoppel, or otherwise, any license or right to (a) make use of any such Confidential Information, or (b) any patents or other intellectual property of FormFactor, other than for the purpose, provided that the Recipient notifies FormFactor within 30 days of the date of the information exchange. Recipient agrees that neither it nor any of its subsidiaries, affiliates or representatives will use Confidential Information for outer than the purpose without the specific and written express consent of FormFactor prior to such use. Furthermore, Recipient agrees that Confidential Information is the sole property of FormFactor and that Recipient has no proprietary interest in such information whatsoever.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause requiring the Recipient to notify FormFactor within 30 days of the information exchange, which seemingly grants a conditional right to use the Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the initial statement in the same section that 'Neither execution of this Agreement nor the furnishing of any Confidential Information to Recipient shall be construed as granting to Recipient... any license or right to... make use of any such Confidential Information... other than for the purpose'. This creates a clear in-text contradiction because the original text explicitly denies any right to use the information, while the modified text implies a conditional right with notification, which contradicts the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', as the different sections provide conflicting obligations.
